title: env-prop-fixture-test — minimal no-op plan for the SI-13 env-propagation smoke test
problem: The SI-11 plan-routing-filter sets FLEET_DEFAULT_SPECIALTY at bringup time when the priority plan's writable_roots are foreign (outside the codex-fleet repo family). Historically the codex CLI scrubbed CODEX_FLEET_* env vars between spawn and prompt-execution, so the spawned worker observed them as empty even though bringup logged them as set. This fixture exists solely to drive scripts/codex-fleet/test/run-env-propagation.sh: it pins writable_roots to a foreign path (/tmp/env-prop-test) so SI-11 fires, declares one trivial no-op subtask so plan-validator + colony plan publish are happy, and lives entirely outside the operator's real plans/ directory.
acceptance_criteria: Bringup with --plan-slug env-prop-fixture-test sets CODEX_FLEET_SPECIALTY=env-prop-fixture-test in every spawned worker pane (foreign writable_roots forces SI-11 routing-filter to pin the specialty).
printenv CODEX_FLEET_SPECIALTY CODEX_FLEET_TIER CODEX_FLEET_AGENT_NAME CODEX_FLEET_WORKER_CWD inside the spawned codex CLI returns non-empty values for all four variables.
run-env-propagation.sh tears down the fixture tmux session and removes /tmp/env-prop-test on exit, leaving no orphan state behind.

tasks.title: [fixture] no-op env-propagation marker
tasks.description: No-op task. This plan exists only to drive the SI-13 env-propagation smoke test in scripts/codex-fleet/test/run-env-propagation.sh. The test brings up a single-pane fleet against this plan with CODEX_FLEET_TMUX_SOCKET=codex-fleet-test, sends printenv to the spawned codex CLI, asserts the four CODEX_FLEET_* vars are populated, and then tears the fleet down. No worker is ever expected to claim or complete this task; the fixture file_scope path lives under the fixture writable_root and is never written.
